title: West Nile Virus found in mosquito test sample in Jamaica Plain
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A mosquito sample from Jamaica Plain tested positive for West Nile Virus, the first city case this summer.
No human West Nile Virus cases have been reported in Boston, with officials urging precautionary measures.
Residents advised to drain standing water and use insect repellent to mitigate mosquito-borne virus risk.

title: Boston man accused of making homemade explosives, threats on MBTA Commuter Rail
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Shane Cautillo, 29, was charged with possession of hoax devices and unlawful possession of fireworks on June 29.
Cautillo was found with suspicious items including a lithium battery and commercial fireworks that caused an explosion.
Authorities banned Cautillo from MBTA transit and set a $10,000 bail; no injuries resulted from the incident.

title: Lawsuit: ICE detained East Boston father despite legal status
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Pineda, a Salvadoran immigrant with legal status, is suing ICE for unlawful detention and racial profiling.
Pineda was detained for two days in inhumane conditions after ICE allegedly targeted him based on his nationality.
The lawsuit seeks damages under the Federal Tort Claims Act, alleging false arrest and severe emotional distress.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Running backs edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots' Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson led a sixth-ranked NFL rushing attack with 128.9 yards per game last season.
Henderson set a franchise rookie record with 5.1 yards per carry, despite postseason struggles with only 76 rushing yards.
Patriots drafted Jam Miller in the seventh round to address depth concerns, with former primary backup Antonio Gibson released.

title: Boston cyclist, a city employee, hit and killed by vehicle on Tremont Street
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A cyclist, Louisa Gag, 36, was struck and killed by a vehicle on Tremont Street in Mission Hill.
The collision occurred at 8:20 a.m., causing a road closure from Brigham Circle to Roxbury Crossing.
The Boston Police Homicide Unit is investigating the incident; the driver stopped at the scene.

title: Massachusetts Senate passes bill targeting addictive social media features for kids
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate passed a bill requiring social media to disable addictive features for minors, with 38 out of 40 senators voting yes.
The bill targets autoplay, infinite scrolling, and late-night notifications, and requires user verification for addictive features.
The legislation also mandates privacy protections for minors and annual reports from social media companies to the Attorney General.

title: Police seize dog who severely injured smaller pup in Chelsea attack caught on video
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Chelsea police seized a dog after a filmed attack left a smaller dog seriously injured on Chestnut Street.
The larger dog was unlicensed and is under a 10-day quarantine as potential law violations are investigated.
The injured service dog, Snoopy Snoop Dog, requires amputation, impacting its service role for owner Liza Henriquez.

title: Mac Jones opens up about his tumultuous time with the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mac Jones, traded to Jacksonville, discusses his challenging tenure with New England Patriots and clashes with Bill Belichick.
Jones said the Patriots' decline began with offensive coordinator Josh McDaniels leaving, causing confusion in play-calling.
Jones felt his trade to Jacksonville was a new beginning after being benched in favor of Bailey Zappe during his final season.

title: 5 takeaways as France defeat Morocco in the last ‘Boston Stadium’ World Cup game, advance to semifinals
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in the World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium, advancing to the semifinals.
Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembélé scored for France, securing their win over Morocco.
The quarterfinal marked the last World Cup game hosted at "Boston Stadium" before it reverted to Gillette Stadium.

title: ‘He hit me’: Pregame France vs. Morocco press conference briefly halted after journalist altercation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A journalist altercation briefly halted a press conference before the France vs. Morocco World Cup quarterfinal in Boston.
FIFA officials intervened to calm two journalists after one accused the other of hitting him during the event.
French coach Didier Deschamps humorously declined to answer further questions due to time constraints after the incident.
